The search for "CBSE Class 12 previous year question papers download 2024 2025 official site" returned the following summary:
To download official CBSE Class 12 previous year question papers and related exam materials, you should use the official CBSE academic website.

### **Official Websites for Downloads**
*   **CBSE Academic Website:** [cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in)
    *   This is the primary portal for all official exam resources[1][2].
    *   Navigate to the **"Question Bank"** or **"Previous Years' Question Papers"** sections to find archived papers[1][3][4].
    *   You can also access **Sample Question Papers (SQP)** and **Marking Schemes** for various years under the "Sample Question Paper" or "Curriculum" sections[1][5][2].

### **Available Resources**
The official portal provides several useful documents for exam preparation:
*   **Previous Year Question Papers:** Includes actual board exam papers from past years[6][7][8].
*   **Sample Question Papers (SQP):** Provided for the current and past academic sessions (e.g., 2024-2025, 2025-2026) to help you understand the latest exam pattern[9][5][2].
*   **Marking Schemes:** These are essential to understand how marks are awarded for different types of answers[1][5].
*   **Model Answers:** You can view "Model Answer by Candidate" files, which show top-scoring answer sheets from previous years to help you understand how to present your answers effectively[10].

### **How to Navigate**
1.  Go to [cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in).
2.  Look for the **"Question Bank"** or **"Board Examinations"** dropdown menus in the navigation bar[1][3].
3.  Select the specific year or category (e.g., Previous Years' Question Papers, Sample Question Papers) you are looking for[9][4].
4.  Browse by subject to download the PDF files directly[8][10][11].

**Tip:** Always ensure you are downloading files from the `nic.in` domain to guarantee they are the official, authentic documents provided by the Central Board of Secondary Education.

The search for "CBSE Class 12 subjects list all streams science commerce arts" returned the following summary:
In the CBSE Class 12 curriculum, students are typically required to choose a combination of subjects based on their chosen stream—Science, Commerce, or Arts (Humanities)[1]. Regardless of the stream, all students must generally study at least two languages (one of which must be English or Hindi)[2].

Below is the general breakdown of subjects by stream:

### **1. Science Stream**
This stream is primarily focused on students aiming for careers in engineering, medicine, or research[3][1].
*   **Core Subjects:** Physics, Chemistry, and either Mathematics or Biology[3].
*   **Common Electives/Additional Subjects:** Computer Science, Informatics Practices, Biotechnology, Engineering Graphics, Physical Education, Fine Arts, and Psychology[3][4].

### **2. Commerce Stream**
This stream is designed for students interested in business, finance, accounting, and economics[3][1].
*   **Core Subjects:** Accountancy, Business Studies, and Economics[3][4].
*   **Common Electives/Additional Subjects:** Mathematics (or Applied Mathematics), Informatics Practices, Entrepreneurship, Physical Education, and Legal Studies[3][4].

### **3. Arts (Humanities) Stream**
The Arts stream covers a broad range of social sciences and creative subjects, suitable for careers in law, journalism, sociology, and more[3][5].
*   **Core Subjects:** History, Political Science, Geography, and Sociology[3][5].
*   **Common Electives/Additional Subjects:** Psychology, Economics, Fine Arts, Home Science, Philosophy, Physical Education, and Legal Studies[3][4].

### **General Scheme of Studies**
*   **Languages:** Students must take two languages (Core or Elective)[2]. Options include English, Hindi, and various regional and foreign languages (e.g., French, German, Tamil, Bengali, etc.)[6][2].
*   **Additional Subjects:** Beyond the core stream subjects, students can often choose a 5th or 6th (additional) subject from the elective list to further their interests[3][2].
*   **Internal Assessments:** All students are also evaluated on mandatory areas such as General Studies, Work Experience, and Physical & Health Education[7][2].

*Note: Schools may have specific combinations available based on their resources. It is recommended to check with your specific school regarding the exact subject groupings they offer.*

You can access Class 12 previous year question papers and sample papers directly from the official CBSE websites.

### **Where to find them:**

*   **For Previous Year Question Papers (Board Exams):**
    The official CBSE examination portal hosts archives of question papers from previous years[1][2].
    *   **Website:** [cbse.gov.in](https://www.cbse.gov.in)
    *   Look for the **"Examination Materials"** or **"Previous Years' Question Papers"** section[1][2]. This area provides access to question papers from various years (e.g., 2012–2020 and beyond)[3][4][5][2].

*   **For Sample Question Papers (SQP) & Marking Schemes:**
    The CBSE Academic website is the designated source for current and past sample question papers and their corresponding marking schemes[6][7].
    *   **Website:** [cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in)
    *   Navigate to the **"Sample Question Paper"** tab[6]. You will find archives categorized by academic year, allowing you to download PDFs for Class XII for sessions ranging from 2014–2015 to the current year[6][8][9][10][11].

### **Quick Tips for Searching:**
*   **Official Sources Only:** Always ensure you are on a `.nic.in` or `.gov.in` domain to avoid unauthorized or outdated materials.
*   **Marking Schemes:** When downloading sample papers, look for the adjacent **"MS"** or **"Marking Scheme"** link[12][8]. These are essential for understanding how to structure your answers to receive full marks.

To download the CBSE Class 12 previous year question papers for 2023, 2024, and 2025, you can use the official CBSE resources and reputable educational platforms.

### **Official Source (CBSE Website)**
The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) provides access to previous years' question papers directly through their academic portal.
*   **Official Website:** Visit the [CBSE Previous Years' Question Papers page](https://www.cbse.gov.in/) (or search for "CBSE Previous Years' Question Papers" on the official `cbse.gov.in` domain)[1][2].
*   **What you can find:** You can download official question papers for board examinations from previous years (including 2023, 2024, and 2025)[1][3]. The portal also provides Marking Schemes which are helpful for understanding how to structure your answers[4][5].

### **Third-Party Educational Resources**
Several educational websites host these papers in an easily accessible, organized PDF format, often accompanied by solutions[6][7][8]:
*   **Vedantu / Shiksha / Oswaal360:** These platforms offer subject-wise previous year question papers (PYQs) for Class 12, often organized by year[6][9][3]. They are useful if you are looking for collections that include solutions or additional study tips[6][10][11].
*   **Mobile Apps:** There are various "CBSE Previous Year Paper" apps available on the Google Play Store that allow for offline access to papers from 2015–2025, which can be convenient for practice[12].

### **Important Tips for Your Preparation**
*   **Distinguish between SQP and PYQ:** When searching, ensure you distinguish between **Sample Question Papers (SQP)**—which are model papers for the upcoming/current academic year—and **Previous Year Question Papers (PYQ)**, which are the actual papers from past board exams[4][13][14].
*   **Use Marking Schemes:** Always download the accompanying marking scheme if available[6][15]. It provides the official criteria for how marks are awarded, which is essential for maximizing your score[10][11].
*   **Latest Syllabus:** While practicing older papers, ensure you check your current syllabus, as topics or exam patterns may occasionally be updated by the CBSE[10][15].

Good. Now let me create a comprehensive download script. I have the CBSE page structure — all papers are ZIP files at `https://www.cbse.gov.in/cbsenew/question-paper/{year}/XII/{filename}.zip`. Let me create a PowerShell script to parse the full page and download everything.

ArtifactMetadata.Summary: PowerShell script to download all CBSE Class XII PYQs from the official cbse.gov.in website. Parses the HTML page for all subject ZIP links, downloads them, extracts into organized subject/year folders at D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\<subject-name>\<year>\

args.CodeContent:
# CBSE Class XII PYQ Downloader
# Downloads all question papers from cbse.gov.in and organizes by subject

$ErrorActionPreference = "Continue"
$baseDir = "D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey"
$baseUrl = "https://www.cbse.gov.in/cbsenew/"
$pageUrl = "https://www.cbse.gov.in/cbsenew/question-paper.html"

# Create base directory
if (-not (Test-Path $baseDir)) {
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $baseDir -Force | Out-Null
}

Write-Host "=== CBSE Class XII PYQ Downloader ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "Target: $baseDir" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host ""

# Step 1: Fetch the question paper page
Write-Host "[1/4] Fetching CBSE question paper page..." -ForegroundColor Green
try {
    $response = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $pageUrl -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 60
    $html = $response.Content
    Write-Host "  Page fetched successfully ($(($html.Length / 1024).ToString('N0')) KB)" -ForegroundColor Gray
} catch {
    Write-Host "  ERROR: Failed to fetch page: $_" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

# Step 2: Parse all ZIP download links for Class XII
Write-Host "[2/4] Parsing download links..." -ForegroundColor Green

# Extract all href links that point to XII ZIP files
$pattern = 'href="(question-paper/\d{4}/XII/[^"]+\.zip)"'
$matches_found = [regex]::Matches($html, $pattern)

# Also try relative paths
$pattern2 = 'href="(question-paper/\d{4}/XII/[^"]+\.zip)"'

$downloads = @()
foreach ($match in $matches_found) {
    $relPath = $match.Groups[1].Value
    $fullUrl = $baseUrl + $relPath
    
    # Extract year and subject from path
    if ($relPath -match 'question-paper/(\d{4})/XII/(.+)\.zip') {
        $year = $Matches[1]
        $subjectFile = $Matches[2]
        
        # Clean up subject name for folder
        $subjectName = $subjectFile -replace '_', ' '
        $subjectName = $subjectName -replace '  +', ' '
        $subjectName = (Get-Culture).TextInfo.ToTitleCase($subjectName.ToLower())
        
        $downloads += [PSCustomObject]@{
            Url = $fullUrl
            Year = $year
            Subject = $subjectName
            FileName = "$subjectFile.zip"
            RelPath = $relPath
        }
    }
}

Write-Host "  Found $($downloads.Count) download links" -ForegroundColor Gray

# Group by year for display
$byYear = $downloads | Group-Object -Property Year
foreach ($yearGroup in $byYear) {
    Write-Host "    Year $($yearGroup.Name): $($yearGroup.Count) subjects" -ForegroundColor Gray
}

# Step 3: Download and extract
Write-Host "[3/4] Downloading and extracting papers..." -ForegroundColor Green

$totalCount = $downloads.Count
$currentCount = 0
$successCount = 0
$failCount = 0
$skipCount = 0

foreach ($dl in $downloads) {
    $currentCount++
    $subjectDir = Join-Path $baseDir $dl.Subject
    $yearDir = Join-Path $subjectDir $dl.Year
    $zipPath = Join-Path $yearDir $dl.FileName
    $extractDir = $yearDir
    
    # Create directory
    if (-not (Test-Path $yearDir)) {
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $yearDir -Force | Out-Null
    }
    
    # Check if already downloaded and extracted
    $extractedFiles = Get-ChildItem -Path $yearDir -Filter "*.pdf" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($extractedFiles.Count -gt 0) {
        Write-Host "  [$currentCount/$totalCount] SKIP $($dl.Subject) ($($dl.Year)) - already extracted" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
        $skipCount++
        continue
    }
    
    Write-Host "  [$currentCount/$totalCount] Downloading $($dl.Subject) ($($dl.Year))..." -ForegroundColor White -NoNewline
    
    try {
        # Download
        Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $dl.Url -OutFile $zipPath -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 120
        
        # Extract
        try {
            Expand-Archive -Path $zipPath -DestinationPath $extractDir -Force
            Remove-Item $zipPath -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
            $extractedCount = (Get-ChildItem -Path $extractDir -Recurse -File).Count
            Write-Host " OK ($extractedCount files)" -ForegroundColor Green
            $successCount++
        } catch {
            Write-Host " EXTRACT ERROR: $_" -ForegroundColor Yellow
            # Keep the ZIP file if extraction fails
            $successCount++  # Still count as success since we got the ZIP
        }
    } catch {
        Write-Host " DOWNLOAD ERROR: $_" -ForegroundColor Red
        $failCount++
        # Clean up empty directories
        if ((Get-ChildItem -Path $yearDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Count -eq 0) {
            Remove-Item $yearDir -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        }
    }
    
    # Small delay to be polite to the server
    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 200
}

# Step 4: Summary
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "[4/4] Download Summary" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "  Total subjects found: $totalCount" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Successfully downloaded: $successCount" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "  Skipped (already exist): $skipCount" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
Write-Host "  Failed: $failCount" -ForegroundColor $(if ($failCount -gt 0) { "Red" } else { "Green" })

# List all subject folders
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Archive Contents:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
$folders = Get-ChildItem -Path $baseDir -Directory | Sort-Object Name
foreach ($folder in $folders) {
    $fileCount = (Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Count
    $sizeBytes = (Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object -Property Length -Sum).Sum
    $sizeMB = if ($sizeBytes) { ($sizeBytes / 1MB).ToString("N1") } else { "0.0" }
    Write-Host "  $($folder.Name): $fileCount files ($sizeMB MB)" -ForegroundColor White
}

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=== Done! Archive saved to: $baseDir ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan
args.Description: Master script to download all CBSE Class XII PYQs from cbse.gov.in, parse the HTML for all subject links across multiple years, download ZIPs, and extract them into subject folders.

    "Beauty _and_Wellness" = "Beauty and Wellness"
    "331_BEAUTY_WELLNESS" = "Beauty and Wellness"
    "Yoga" = "Yoga"
    "365_Yoga" = "Yoga"
    "Early_Childhood_Care_Educaiton" = "Early Childhood Care and Education"
    "Early_Childhood_Care_&_Education" = "Early Childhood Care and Education"
    "Early_Childhood_Care_Edn" = "Early Childhood Care and Education"
    "366_EARLY_CHILDHOOD_CARE_EDUCATION" = "Early Childhood Care and Education"
    "National_Cadet_Corps_NCC" = "National Cadet Corps (NCC)"
    "NCC" = "National Cadet Corps (NCC)"
    "42_National_Cadet_Corps_NCC" = "National Cadet Corps (NCC)"
    "Knowledge_Tradubg" = "Knowledge Traditions and Practices of India"
    "KTPI" = "Knowledge Traditions and Practices of India"
    "39_KTPI" = "Knowledge Traditions and Practices of India"
    "Library_and_Information_Science" = "Library and Information Science"
    "Library_Info_Science" = "Library and Information Science"
    "Library_Information_Science" = "Library and Information Science"
    "360_Library_Information_Science" = "Library and Information Science"
    "Mass_Media_Studies" = "Mass Media Studies"
    "359_Mass_Media_Studies" = "Mass Media Studies"
    "Textile_Design" = "Textile Design"
    "353_Textile_Design" = "Textile Design"
    "Design" = "Design"
    "354_Design" = "Design"
}

# Create base directory
if (-not (Test-Path $baseDir)) {
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $baseDir -Force | Out-Null
}

Write-Host "=== CBSE Class XII PYQ Complete Downloader ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "Target: $baseDir" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "Batch: $BatchId" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host ""

# Step 1: Fetch the question paper page
Write-Host "[1/4] Fetching CBSE question paper page..." -ForegroundColor Green
try {
    $response = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $pageUrl -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 60
    $html = $response.Content
    Write-Host "  Page fetched successfully" -ForegroundColor Gray
} catch {
    Write-Host "  ERROR: Failed to fetch page: $_" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

# Step 2: Parse all ZIP download links for Class XII
Write-Host "[2/4] Parsing download links..." -ForegroundColor Green

$zipPattern = 'href="(question-paper/\d{4}/XII/[^"]+\.zip)"'
$zipMatches = [regex]::Matches($html, $zipPattern)

$downloads = @()
foreach ($match in $zipMatches) {
    $relPath = $match.Groups[1].Value
    $fullUrl = $baseUrl + $relPath
    
    if ($relPath -match 'question-paper/(\d{4})/XII/(.+)\.zip') {
        $year = $Matches[1]
        $subjectFile = $Matches[2]
        
        # Normalize subject name
        $subjectName = $subjectFile.Trim()
        if ($subjectNormMap.ContainsKey($subjectName)) {
            $subjectName = $subjectNormMap[$subjectName]
        } else {
            # Fallback: clean up the name
            $subjectName = $subjectName -replace '^\d+_', ''
            $subjectName = $subjectName -replace '_', ' '
            $subjectName = $subjectName -replace '  +', ' '
            $subjectName = (Get-Culture).TextInfo.ToTitleCase($subjectName.ToLower().Trim())
        }
        
        $downloads += [PSCustomObject]@{
            Url = $fullUrl
            Year = $year
            Subject = $subjectName
            FileName = "$subjectFile.zip"
            RelPath = $relPath
        }
    }
}

Write-Host "  Found $($downloads.Count) total download links" -ForegroundColor Gray

# Split into batches if needed
$totalBatches = 4
$batchSize = [Math]::Ceiling($downloads.Count / $totalBatches)

if ($BatchId -ne "all") {
    $batchNum = [int]$BatchId
    $startIdx = ($batchNum - 1) * $batchSize
    $endIdx = [Math]::Min($startIdx + $batchSize, $downloads.Count)
    $downloads = $downloads[$startIdx..($endIdx - 1)]
    Write-Host "  Processing batch $BatchId ($($downloads.Count) items, index $startIdx to $($endIdx - 1))" -ForegroundColor Yellow
}

# Group by year for display
$byYear = $downloads | Group-Object -Property Year
foreach ($yearGroup in $byYear) {
    Write-Host "    Year $($yearGroup.Name): $($yearGroup.Count) subjects" -ForegroundColor Gray
}

# Step 3: Download and extract
Write-Host "[3/4] Downloading and extracting papers..." -ForegroundColor Green

$totalCount = $downloads.Count
$currentCount = 0
$successCount = 0
$failCount = 0
$skipCount = 0
$failedItems = @()

foreach ($dl in $downloads) {
    $currentCount++
    $subjectDir = Join-Path $baseDir $dl.Subject
    $yearDir = Join-Path $subjectDir $dl.Year
    $zipPath = Join-Path $yearDir $dl.FileName
    
    # Create directory
    if (-not (Test-Path $yearDir)) {
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $yearDir -Force | Out-Null
    }
    
    # Check if already downloaded and extracted
    $existingFiles = Get-ChildItem -Path $yearDir -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($existingFiles.Count -gt 0) {
        Write-Host "  [$currentCount/$totalCount] SKIP $($dl.Subject) ($($dl.Year)) - already has $($existingFiles.Count) files" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
        $skipCount++
        continue
    }
    
    Write-Host "  [$currentCount/$totalCount] $($dl.Subject) ($($dl.Year))..." -ForegroundColor White -NoNewline
    
    try {
        # Download with retry
        $retries = 3
        $downloaded = $false
        for ($r = 0; $r -lt $retries; $r++) {
            try {
                Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $dl.Url -OutFile $zipPath -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 180
                $downloaded = $true
                break
            } catch {
                if ($r -lt $retries - 1) {
                    Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
                } else {
                    throw $_
                }
            }
        }
        
        if ($downloaded) {
            # Extract
            try {
                Expand-Archive -Path $zipPath -DestinationPath $yearDir -Force
                Remove-Item $zipPath -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                $extractedCount = (Get-ChildItem -Path $yearDir -Recurse -File).Count
                Write-Host " OK ($extractedCount files)" -ForegroundColor Green
                $successCount++
            } catch {
                Write-Host " EXTRACT WARN (keeping ZIP)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
                $successCount++
            }
        }
    } catch {
        Write-Host " FAILED" -ForegroundColor Red
        $failCount++
        $failedItems += "$($dl.Subject) ($($dl.Year)): $($dl.Url)"
        # Clean up empty directories
        if ((Get-ChildItem -Path $yearDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Count -eq 0) {
            Remove-Item $yearDir -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        }
        if ((Test-Path $subjectDir) -and (Get-ChildItem -Path $subjectDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Count -eq 0) {
            Remove-Item $subjectDir -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        }
    }
    
    # Small delay to be polite to the server
    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 300
}

# Step 4: Summary
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "[4/4] Download Summary (Batch: $BatchId)" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "  Total links processed: $totalCount" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Successfully downloaded: $successCount" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "  Skipped (already exist): $skipCount" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
if ($failCount -gt 0) {
    Write-Host "  Failed: $failCount" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "  Failed items:" -ForegroundColor Red
    foreach ($fi in $failedItems) {
        Write-Host "    - $fi" -ForegroundColor Red
    }
} else {
    Write-Host "  Failed: 0" -ForegroundColor Green
}

# List all subject folders
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=== Current Archive Contents ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan
$folders = Get-ChildItem -Path $baseDir -Directory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Sort-Object Name
$totalFiles = 0
$totalSize = 0
foreach ($folder in $folders) {
    $files = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    $fileCount = $files.Count
    $sizeBytes = ($files | Measure-Object -Property Length -Sum -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Sum
    if (-not $sizeBytes) { $sizeBytes = 0 }
    $sizeMB = ($sizeBytes / 1MB).ToString("N1")
    $years = (Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Directory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Name) -join ", "
    Write-Host "  $($folder.Name): $fileCount files ($sizeMB MB) [$years]" -ForegroundColor White
    $totalFiles += $fileCount
    $totalSize += $sizeBytes
}
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "  TOTAL: $($folders.Count) subjects, $totalFiles files, $(($totalSize / 1MB).ToString('N1')) MB" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "=== Done! ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan
args.Description: Complete CBSE PYQ downloader with subject name normalization across years, retry logic, batch support for parallel downloads, and comprehensive archive reporting.

To download official CBSE Class 12 previous year question papers for the years 2015–2020, you can access them directly through the official CBSE academic website[1][2].

### **How to Access Official Question Papers**
1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the [CBSE Examination Materials](https://www.cbse.gov.in/) portal or [CBSE Academic website](https://cbseacademic.nic.in/)[1][3].
2.  **Navigate to Examination Materials:** Look for the section labeled **"Examination Materials"** or **"Previous Year Question Papers"**[1][3][2].
3.  **Select the Year and Class:** You will find archives categorized by year (e.g., 2020, 2019, 2018, etc.)[1][3]. Choose the specific year, then select **Class XII**[1][2].
4.  **Download PDFs:** A list of subjects will appear. Click on the desired subject to download the question paper in PDF format[4][3].

### **Key Resources Summary**
*   **Official Archive:** The CBSE portal provides question papers, marking schemes, and sometimes model answers for board examinations from 2015 onwards[1][2].
*   **Marking Schemes:** Along with the question papers, it is highly recommended to download the corresponding **Marking Scheme** files, as they provide the official answer keys and step-by-step evaluation criteria[1][5][2].
*   **Third-Party Platforms:** While the official website is the primary source, several educational platforms (such as Vedantu, Byju's, etc.) also compile these papers into organized, subject-wise PDFs with solutions for easier access[6][7][5].

You can download CBSE Class 12 compartment examination question papers directly from the official CBSE website and other educational portals.

### **How to Download from the Official CBSE Website**
The official CBSE website maintains an archive of previous years' question papers, including those for compartment (supplementary) examinations[1][2].

1.  Visit the official CBSE website at **[cbse.gov.in](https://www.cbse.gov.in)**.
2.  Navigate to the **"Examinations"** or **"Academic"** section[2].
3.  Look for the **"Previous Years' Question Papers"** link[3][4].
4.  Select **"Class XII"** and then browse for the specific year (e.g., 2024, 2023, 2022)[3][4].
5.  Look for files labeled **"Compartment Examination"**[3][4].
6.  Click on the subject name to download the PDF file[3].

### **Additional Resources**
If you have difficulty locating a specific paper on the main CBSE site, the following types of resources often host these PDFs for easier access:

*   **Educational Portals:** Websites like *Jagran Josh*, *Collegedunia*, and *Education Observer* frequently compile these papers year-wise and subject-wise for students[5][6][7].
*   **Search Tips:** You can perform a targeted search on Google using the format: 
    *   *"CBSE Class 12 [Subject Name] compartment question paper 2023 PDF"*
    *   *"CBSE Class 12 compartment paper 2022 download"*

**Note:** Always prioritize the official CBSE website (cbse.gov.in) to ensure you are downloading the authentic and correct question papers[2]. If you are looking for marking schemes to check your answers, those are also typically available in the same section on the official website[8][1].

[Message] timestamp=2026-06-14T12:54:57Z sender=235853cb-5da2-4b41-ac03-142420523354 priority=MESSAGE_PRIORITY_HIGH content=## CBSE Class 12 Compartment & Additional Paper Types — Research Report

I performed all 8 searches requested. Here are the comprehensive findings:

---

### 1. COMPARTMENT EXAM QUESTION PAPERS — Sources & Download URLs

#### A. Official CBSE Sources (Primary)

| Source | URL | What's Available |
|--------|-----|------------------|
| **CBSE Main Website — Question Papers Page** | `https://www.cbse.gov.in/cbsenew/question-paper.html` | Archive of previous years' question papers categorized by year AND exam type (Main vs. Compartment). Has specific sections labeled **"Question Papers for Compartment Examination"** for both Class X and XII. PDFs available for download. |
| **CBSE Academic Website** | `https://cbseacademic.nic.in` | Hosts question papers, marking schemes, and SQPs. Navigate to "Question Papers" or "Previous Years' Question Papers" → Select "Class XII" → Select "Compartment Examination [Year]". |
| **CBSE Academic — SQP 2025-26 (Class XII)** | `https://cbseacademic.nic.in/SQP_CLASSXII_2025-26.html` | Latest Sample Question Papers and Marking Schemes for the 2025-26 session (for 2026 board exams). Subject-wise SQP and MS PDF downloads available. |

**Key Finding**: The official question-paper.html page on cbse.gov.in **DOES have compartment sections**. Papers are categorized under "Compartment Examination" for specific years (2024, 2023, 2022, etc.). The compartment exam (now officially called **"Supplementary Examination"**) follows the exact same pattern, syllabus, and marking scheme as the main board exam.

#### B. Third-Party Sources (Secondary — Host Official PDFs)

| Source | URL Domain | Notes |
|--------|-----------|-------|
| **Education Observer** | `educationobserver.com` | Collections of previous years' compartment papers, organized by subject/year |
| **Jagran Josh** | `jagranjosh.com` | Categorized archives of Class 12 board + compartment papers with solutions |
| **Vedantu** | `vedantu.com` | Hosts compartment papers with solutions |
| **Educart** | `educart.co` | PYQs including compartment papers + marking schemes |
| **Collegedunia** | `collegedunia.com` | Structured access to PYQs including compartment |
| **SelfStudys** | `selfstudys.com` | Free PDF downloads of compartment papers |
| **Scribd** | `scribd.com` | Some compartment papers available |

---

### 2. COMPARTMENT PAPERS BY YEAR (2024, 2023, 2022)

- **Available on cbse.gov.in**: Yes — archives on the question-paper page contain compartment papers for multiple years including 2024, 2023, 2022 and earlier.
- **Access path**: `cbse.gov.in` → Academic Website → Question Papers → Previous Years' → Compartment Examination → Select Year → Select Subject → Download PDF
- **Marking schemes** are typically in a separate section on the same portal.

---

### 3. CBSE.GOV.IN COMPARTMENT SECTIONS

**Confirmed**: `https://www.cbse.gov.in/cbsenew/question-paper.html` **does contain compartment sections**.
- The page has categorized links for "Previous Years' Question Papers"
- Specific sections labeled **"Question Papers for Compartment Examination"** exist
- Both Class X and Class XII compartment papers are available
- PDFs are downloadable for various subjects

---

### 4. IMPROVEMENT EXAM PAPERS

**Important Finding**: There is **NO separate category** for "improvement exam" papers. The improvement exam uses the **exact same question paper format and syllabus** as the regular main board examination. Therefore:
- Students preparing for improvement exams should use the **regular board examination previous year papers**
- Available at: `cbse.gov.in` → Academic/Board Examinations → Previous Years' Question Papers
- Same papers from `cbseacademic.nic.in` apply

---

### 5. OFFICIAL PRACTICE PAPERS

| Resource | URL | Details |
|----------|-----|---------|
| **Sample Question Papers (SQP)** | `https://cbseacademic.nic.in` → "Sample Question Paper" tab | Official SQPs + Marking Schemes (MS), organized by academic year and class. Current: 2025-26 session. |
| **SQP Class XII 2025-26** | `https://cbseacademic.nic.in/SQP_CLASSXII_2025-26.html` | Direct link for Class XII sample papers for 2026 board exams |
| **Question Banks** | `https://cbseacademic.nic.in` → "Question Bank" tab | Official question banks + "Additional Practice Questions" |
| **Previous Years' Papers** | `https://www.cbse.gov.in` → Board Examination archives | Real exam papers from past years |

---

### 6. MODEL ANSWER PAPERS

| Resource | URL | Details |
|----------|-----|---------|
| **Model Answer by Candidate (Topper Sheets)** | `https://cbseacademic.nic.in` → "Model Answers" tab | Actual handwritten answer sheets of top-performing students. Categorized by year (2024, 2023, 2020, etc.). Subjects: Accountancy, Business Studies, Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Mathematics, and more. |
| **Also accessible via** | `https://www.cbse.gov.in` → Academics/Examination → Model Answers | Same content, different navigation path |

**Purpose**: These show how top students presented answers — useful for answer-writing technique, formatting, step-wise marking understanding.

**Note on personal answer sheets**: Getting a photocopy of your *own* answer sheet is a separate post-result process via the CBSE Student Portal.

---

### 7. CBSE MODEL-ANSWER.HTML PAGE

The Model Answers section on `cbse.gov.in` provides:
- Links categorized by year of examination (2024, 2023, 2020, etc.)
- Click desired year → Class XII → Subject-wise PDF downloads
- Covers multiple subjects across Science, Commerce, and Humanities streams
- These are **actual topper answer sheets** (handwritten, scanned)

---

### 8. CBSE AICE SAMPLE PAPERS

**Key Finding**: There is **NO "CBSE AICE" exam**. The searches clarify:
- **AISSCE** (All India Senior School Certificate Examination) = the formal/official name for the CBSE Class 12 Board Exam. Sample papers for this are available at `cbseacademic.nic.in`.
- **Cambridge AICE Diploma** (Advanced International Certificate of Education) = a completely separate international qualification from Cambridge International Education, NOT part of CBSE.
- If "AICE" was intended as "AISSCE", all the sample papers mentioned above (at `cbseacademic.nic.in`) apply.

---

### SUMMARY OF ALL KEY DOWNLOADABLE URLs

| # | Resource Type | Primary URL |
|---|--------------|-------------|
| 1 | **Question Papers (Main + Compartment)** | `https://www.cbse.gov.in/cbsenew/question-paper.html` |
| 2 | **CBSE Academic Portal (SQP, Question Banks, Model Answers)** | `https://cbseacademic.nic.in` |
| 3 | **Sample Question Papers Class XII 2025-26** | `https://cbseacademic.nic.in/SQP_CLASSXII_2025-26.html` |
| 4 | **Model Answer Sheets (Topper)** | `https://cbseacademic.nic.in` → Model Answers section |
| 5 | **CBSE Main Portal** | `https://www.cbse.gov.in` |

**Third-party aggregators**: educationobserver.com, jagranjosh.com, vedantu.com, educart.co, collegedunia.com, selfstudys.com — all host copies of official CBSE papers organized for easier access.

You can download CBSE Class 12 previous year question papers for 2017–2021, along with other years, from various educational platforms and the official CBSE website[1][2][3][4].

### **Where to Download**

*   **Vedantu & SelfStudys:** Both websites provide comprehensive archives of CBSE Class 12 previous year question papers, often including solutions[5][6][2].
    *   **Vedantu:** Offers subject-wise question papers and solutions[7][8]. You can search their "Previous Year Question Papers" section to filter by year[5][9].
    *   **SelfStudys:** Provides papers chapter-wise and year-wise, which can be useful for focused study[10].
*   **Official CBSE Website:** You can access official question papers directly from the [CBSE Academic website](https://www.cbse.gov.in/) under the "Question Papers for Examination" section[3].

### **How to Access the PDFs**
1.  **Visit the Websites:** Go to [Vedantu](https://www.vedantu.com) or [SelfStudys](https://www.selfstudys.com) and navigate to the "Previous Year Question Papers" or "CBSE" section[5][2].
2.  **Filter by Class and Subject:** Select "Class 12" and then choose your desired subject (e.g., Physics, Chemistry, Maths, English)[2][7].
3.  **Select the Year:** Look for the year filters (2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021) to download the specific PDF files[4][11].
4.  **Download:** Most of these platforms allow you to download the PDFs for free, sometimes requiring a simple sign-up or directly providing a download link[1][11].

**Note:** Please be aware that there were no board examinations in the 2020-21 academic year due to the COVID-19 pandemic, so you may find sample papers or alternative assessment materials for that period instead[10].

To download the official CBSE Class 12 Question Bank and related academic resources for the 2025-26 session, you can follow these steps on the official website:

### **Steps to Download**
1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to [cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in).
2.  **Navigate to the Question Bank Section:** Look for the **"Question Bank"** link in the main menu or under the "Publications" / "Academic" section[1][2][3].
3.  **Select Your Class:** Choose **"Class XII"** from the available options[2][4].
4.  **Download Subjects:** You will see a list of subjects. Click on the subject you need to download the corresponding PDF file[5][6].

### **Important Notes for 2025-26**
*   **Sample Question Papers (SQPs):** In addition to the Question Bank, CBSE has released the **Sample Question Papers (SQP) and Marking Schemes (MS)** for the 2025-26 academic session[7][8]. These are highly recommended for understanding the current exam pattern and marking scheme[5][6].
*   **Assessment Scheme:** The assessment scheme for the 2025-26 session remains the same as the previous year (2024-25)[9][8].
*   **Official Resources:** Always rely on the `cbseacademic.nic.in` website for authentic materials, as third-party websites may contain outdated or unofficial content[5][10].

To access the CBSE Class 12 question banks and sample papers for the 2025-26 academic session, you should use the official CBSE academic website[1][2].

### **How to Access Official Materials**
1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to [cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in)[3][4].
2.  **Navigate to the Relevant Section:**
    *   **For Sample Question Papers (SQP):** Look for the "Sample Question Paper" or "SQP 2025-2026" link on the homepage[1][4].
    *   **For Question Banks:** Look for the "Question Bank" tab in the main menu[5][6].
3.  **Select Your Class and Subject:** Once in the respective section, select **"Class XII"** and then choose the specific subject you need to download the PDF[3][4].

### **Important Notes**
*   **Availability:** CBSE provides official Sample Question Papers (SQPs) and Marking Schemes (MS) for the 2025-26 session on the portal[7][4]. Ensure you are downloading the files labeled for the **2025-26** session to get the most accurate and current curriculum-aligned content[8][9].
*   **Distinction:** Note that "Question Banks" and "Sample Question Papers" are different resources[3][10].
    *   **Sample Papers:** Provide the format, marking scheme, and structure of the board exam[3][11].
    *   **Question Banks:** Often contain a larger collection of practice questions designed to test competency and conceptual understanding[3][12].
*   **Direct URLs:** While direct PDF links change frequently, the official landing page for these resources is consistently [cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in)[3][6]. Avoid third-party websites that may provide outdated or unofficial materials.

To access and download the **Additional Practice Questions** from the official CBSE website, please follow these steps:

### How to Download
1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to [cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in)[1].
2.  **Navigate to the Resource Section:** Look for the **"Question Bank"** tab or the **"Skill Education"** section in the main menu[2][1].
3.  **Locate Additional Practice Questions:** Within the menu, you will typically find a direct link labeled **"Additional Practice Questions"** for Class X and XII[2][3][1].
4.  **Select Your Class:** Choose **Class XII** to view the list of available subjects[1].
5.  **Download PDF:** Click on the subject name to open or download the PDF file[4].

### Important Notes
*   **Availability:** CBSE typically releases "Additional Practice Questions" to provide extra competency-based practice for board exam aspirants[5][4]. Note that these are supplementary to the standard "Sample Question Papers" (SQP)[2][6].
*   **Session Specificity:** The official website organizes materials by academic session[2]. Ensure you are looking under the section for the relevant academic year (e.g., 2024-25) if available, as older materials (such as 2023-24) are often kept in the archives[2][7].
*   **Official Sources:** Always prefer [cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in) for these documents to ensure you are downloading the authentic, board-issued material[1]. Avoid third-party websites unless you are verifying against the official CBSE portal[8][4].

foreach ($folder in $folders) {
    $folderName = $folder.Name
    
    if ($folderMergeMap.ContainsKey($folderName)) {
        $canonicalName = $folderMergeMap[$folderName]
        
        if ($null -eq $canonicalName) {
            # Delete empty/unwanted folders
            $files = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
            if ($files.Count -eq 0) {
                Write-Host "  DELETE (empty): $folderName" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
                Remove-Item $folder.FullName -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                $deletedCount++
            }
            continue
        }
        
        if ($folderName -ne $canonicalName) {
            $targetDir = Join-Path $baseDir $canonicalName
            
            if (-not (Test-Path $targetDir)) {
                # Simple rename
                Rename-Item -Path $folder.FullName -NewName $canonicalName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                Write-Host "  RENAME: $folderName -> $canonicalName" -ForegroundColor Yellow
                $mergedCount++
            } else {
                # Merge into existing folder
                $subDirs = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Directory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                foreach ($subDir in $subDirs) {
                    $destSubDir = Join-Path $targetDir $subDir.Name
                    if (-not (Test-Path $destSubDir)) {
                        Move-Item -Path $subDir.FullName -Destination $destSubDir -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                    } else {
                        # Move individual files
                        $files = Get-ChildItem -Path $subDir.FullName -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                        foreach ($file in $files) {
                            $destFile = Join-Path $destSubDir $file.Name
                            if (-not (Test-Path $destFile)) {
                                Move-Item -Path $file.FullName -Destination $destFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
                
                # Also move any root-level files
                $rootFiles = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                foreach ($rf in $rootFiles) {
                    $destFile = Join-Path $targetDir $rf.Name
                    if (-not (Test-Path $destFile)) {
                        Move-Item -Path $rf.FullName -Destination $destFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                    }
                }
                
                # Remove source if empty
                $remaining = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                if ($remaining.Count -eq 0) {
                    Remove-Item $folder.FullName -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                    Write-Host "  MERGE: $folderName -> $canonicalName (folder removed)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
                } else {
                    Write-Host "  MERGE: $folderName -> $canonicalName (some files remain)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
                }
                $mergedCount++
            }
        }
    }
}

Write-Host "  Merged: $mergedCount folders, Deleted: $deletedCount empty folders" -ForegroundColor Gray

# Step 2: Remove empty directories
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "[2/3] Cleaning empty directories..." -ForegroundColor Green

$emptyCount = 0
$folders = Get-ChildItem -Path $baseDir -Directory -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Sort-Object { $_.FullName.Length } -Descending
foreach ($folder in $folders) {
    $items = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($items.Count -eq 0) {
        Remove-Item $folder.FullName -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        $emptyCount++
    }
}
Write-Host "  Removed $emptyCount empty directories" -ForegroundColor Gray

# Step 3: Generate final report
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "[3/3] Final Archive Report" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  CBSE Class XII PYQ Archive" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  Location: $baseDir" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "=============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

$folders = Get-ChildItem -Path $baseDir -Directory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Sort-Object Name
$totalFiles = 0
$totalSize = 0
$report = @()

foreach ($folder in $folders) {
    $files = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    $fileCount = $files.Count
    $sizeBytes = ($files | Measure-Object -Property Length -Sum -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Sum
    if (-not $sizeBytes) { $sizeBytes = 0 }
    $sizeMB = ($sizeBytes / 1MB).ToString("N1")
    $years = (Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Directory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Name | Sort-Object) -join ", "
    
    Write-Host "  $($folder.Name)" -ForegroundColor White
    Write-Host "    Files: $fileCount | Size: $sizeMB MB | Contents: $years" -ForegroundColor Gray
    
    $totalFiles += $fileCount
    $totalSize += $sizeBytes
    
    $report += [PSCustomObject]@{
        Subject = $folder.Name
        Files = $fileCount
        SizeMB = [math]::Round($sizeBytes / 1MB, 1)
        Years = $years
    }
}

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  TOTALS" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  Subjects: $($folders.Count)" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Files: $totalFiles" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Size: $(($totalSize / 1MB).ToString('N1')) MB ($(($totalSize / 1GB).ToString('N2')) GB)" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "=============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
args.Description: Cleanup script to consolidate variant SQP folder names into canonical subject names, remove empty directories, and generate a comprehensive archive report.

args.CodeContent:
# Comprehensive Cleanup and Consolidation of CBSE PYQ Archive
# Merges ALL variant SQP folder names into canonical subject names

$ErrorActionPreference = "Continue"
$baseDir = "D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey"

Write-Host "=== CBSE Archive Cleanup & Consolidation ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan

# Complete map of ALL variant names to canonical names (from batch outputs)
$folderMergeMap = @{
    # SQP naming variants -> Canonical
    "Acc" = "Accountancy"
    "Bharatnatyam" = "Bharatanatyam Dance"
    "Bharatnatyam Ms Hi" = "Bharatanatyam Dance"
    "Bharatnatyam Sqp Hi" = "Bharatanatyam Dance"
    "Biology Ms Hi" = "Biology"
    "Biology Sqp Hi" = "Biology"
    "Biotechnology Ms Hi" = "Biotechnology"
    "Biotechnology Sqp Hi" = "Biotechnology"
    "Businessstudies" = "Business Studies"
    "Carnaticmusicvocal" = "Carnatic Music (Vocal)"
    "Carnaticmusicmelodicinstrument" = "Carnatic Music (Melodic Instruments)"
    "Carnaticmusicpercussion" = "Carnatic Music (Percussion Instruments)"
    "Chemistry Ms Hi" = "Chemistry"
    "Chemistry Sqp Hi" = "Chemistry"
    "Computerscience" = "Computer Science"
    "Engggraphics" = "Engineering Graphics"
    "Englishcore" = "English Core"
    "Englishelective" = "English Elective"
    "Geography Ms Hi" = "Geography"
    "Geography Sqp Hi" = "Geography"
    "Graphic" = "Graphics (Fine Art)"
    "Graphic Ms Hi" = "Graphics (Fine Art)"
    "Graphic Sqp Hi" = "Graphics (Fine Art)"
    "Hindicore" = "Hindi Core"
    "Hindielective" = "Hindi Elective"
    "Hindustanivocal" = "Hindustani Music (Vocal)"
    "Hindustanimelodic" = "Hindustani Music (Melodic Instruments)"
    "Hindustanipercussion" = "Hindustani Music (Percussion Instruments)"
    "History Ms Hi" = "History"
    "History Sqp Hi" = "History"
    "Homescience" = "Home Science"
    "Homescience Ms Hi" = "Home Science"
    "Homescience Sqp Hi" = "Home Science"
    "Informaticspractices" = "Informatics Practices"
    "Kathak" = "Kathak Dance"
    "Kathak Ms Hi" = "Kathak Dance"
    "Kathak Sqp Hi" = "Kathak Dance"
    "Kathakali" = "Kathakali Dance"
    "Kathakali Ms Hi" = "Kathakali Dance"
    "Kathakali Sqp Hi" = "Kathakali Dance"
    "Kokborok Ms" = "Kokborok"
    "Kokborok Sqp" = "Kokborok"
    "Ktpi" = "Knowledge Traditions and Practices of India"
    "Kuchipudi" = "Kuchipudi Dance"
    "Kuchipudi Ms Hi" = "Kuchipudi Dance"
    "Kuchipudi Sqp Hi" = "Kuchipudi Dance"
    "Legalstudies" = "Legal Studies"
    "Legalstudies Ms Hi" = "Legal Studies"
    "Legalstudies Sqp Hi" = "Legal Studies"
    "Library" = "Library and Information Science"
    "Manipuridance" = "Manipuri Dance"
    "Manipuridance Ms Hi" = "Manipuri Dance"
    "Manipuridance Sqp Hi" = "Manipuri Dance"
    "Maths Ms Hi" = "Mathematics"
    "Maths Sqp Hi" = "Mathematics"
    "Ncc" = "National Cadet Corps (NCC)"
    "Odissi" = "Odissi Dance"
    "Odissi Ms Hi" = "Odissi Dance"
    "Odissi Sqp Hi" = "Odissi Dance"
    "Painting Ms Hi" = "Painting"
    "Painting Sqp Hi" = "Painting"
    "Physcaleducation" = "Physical Education"
    "Physicaleducation" = "Physical Education"
    "Physics Ms Hi" = "Physics"
    "Physics Sqp Hi" = "Physics"
    "Polsci" = "Political Science"
    "Polsci Ms Hi" = "Political Science"
    "Polsci Sqp Hi" = "Political Science"
    "Sanskritcore" = "Sanskrit Core"
    "Sanskrit Core Ms Core" = "Sanskrit Core"
    "Sanskrit Core Sqp Core" = "Sanskrit Core"
    "Sanskritelective" = "Sanskrit Elective"
    "Sculpture Ms Hi" = "Sculpture"
    "Sculpture Sqp Hi" = "Sculpture"
    "Sociology Ms Hi" = "Sociology"
    "Sociology Sqp Hi" = "Sociology"
    "Tangkhulmil" = "Tangkhul"
    "Teluguap" = "Telugu"
    "Telugutl" = "Telugu (Telangana)"
    "Urducore" = "Urdu Core"
    "Urduelective" = "Urdu Elective"
    
    # Empty/broken folders to delete
    "Hrgs" = "__DELETE__"
    "Mathsvic" = "__DELETE__"
    "Multimedia Webtech Ms 2018 19" = "__DELETE__"
    "Multimedia Webtech Sqp 2018 19" = "__DELETE__"
}

Write-Host "[1/3] Merging variant folders..." -ForegroundColor Green

$mergedCount = 0; $deletedCount = 0

# Process folders - need to re-read each time since we're modifying
$processedAny = $true
while ($processedAny) {
    $processedAny = $false
    $folders = Get-ChildItem -Path $baseDir -Directory -EA SilentlyContinue | Sort-Object Name
    
    foreach ($folder in $folders) {
        $folderName = $folder.Name
        
        if (-not $folderMergeMap.ContainsKey($folderName)) { continue }
        
        $canonicalName = $folderMergeMap[$folderName]
        
        if ($canonicalName -eq "__DELETE__") {
            $fileCount = (Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Recurse -File -EA SilentlyContinue).Count
            if ($fileCount -eq 0) {
                Remove-Item $folder.FullName -Recurse -Force -EA SilentlyContinue
                Write-Host "  DELETE: $folderName (empty)" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
                $deletedCount++
                $processedAny = $true
            }
            continue
        }
        
        if ($folderName -eq $canonicalName) { continue }
        
        $targetDir = Join-Path $baseDir $canonicalName
        
        if (-not (Test-Path $targetDir)) {
            Rename-Item -Path $folder.FullName -NewName $canonicalName -EA SilentlyContinue
            Write-Host "  RENAME: $folderName -> $canonicalName" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        } else {
            # Merge contents
            $subDirs = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Directory -EA SilentlyContinue
            foreach ($subDir in $subDirs) {
                $destSubDir = Join-Path $targetDir $subDir.Name
                if (-not (Test-Path $destSubDir)) {
                    Move-Item -Path $subDir.FullName -Destination $destSubDir -Force -EA SilentlyContinue
                } else {
                    Get-ChildItem -Path $subDir.FullName -File -EA SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object {
                        $destFile = Join-Path $destSubDir $_.Name
                        if (-not (Test-Path $destFile)) {
                            Move-Item -Path $_.FullName -Destination $destFile -Force -EA SilentlyContinue
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
            # Move root files too
            Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -File -EA SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object {
                $destFile = Join-Path $targetDir $_.Name
                if (-not (Test-Path $destFile)) { Move-Item -Path $_.FullName -Destination $destFile -Force -EA SilentlyContinue }
            }
            # Remove if empty
            $remaining = (Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Recurse -File -EA SilentlyContinue).Count
            if ($remaining -eq 0) {
                Remove-Item $folder.FullName -Recurse -Force -EA SilentlyContinue
                Write-Host "  MERGE: $folderName -> $canonicalName" -ForegroundColor Yellow
            } else {
                Write-Host "  PARTIAL MERGE: $folderName -> $canonicalName ($remaining files remain)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
            }
        }
        $mergedCount++
        $processedAny = $true
        break  # Restart loop since folder list changed
    }
}

Write-Host "  Merged: $mergedCount, Deleted: $deletedCount" -ForegroundColor Gray

# Step 2: Remove empty directories (bottom-up)
Write-Host "[2/3] Cleaning empty directories..." -ForegroundColor Green
$emptyCount = 0
do {
    $found = $false
    Get-ChildItem -Path $baseDir -Directory -Recurse -EA SilentlyContinue | Sort-Object { $_.FullName.Length } -Descending | ForEach-Object {
        if ((Get-ChildItem -Path $_.FullName -EA SilentlyContinue).Count -eq 0) {
            Remove-Item $_.FullName -Force -EA SilentlyContinue
            $emptyCount++
            $found = $true
        }
    }
} while ($found)
Write-Host "  Removed $emptyCount empty directories" -ForegroundColor Gray

# Step 3: Final report
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  CBSE Class XII PYQ Complete Archive" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  Location: $baseDir" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "=============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

$folders = Get-ChildItem -Path $baseDir -Directory -EA SilentlyContinue | Sort-Object Name
$totalFiles = 0; $totalSize = 0

foreach ($folder in $folders) {
    $files = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Recurse -File -EA SilentlyContinue
    $fileCount = $files.Count
    $sizeBytes = ($files | Measure-Object -Property Length -Sum -EA SilentlyContinue).Sum
    if (-not $sizeBytes) { $sizeBytes = 0 }
    $sizeMB = ($sizeBytes / 1MB).ToString("N1")
    $years = (Get-ChildItem -Path $folder.FullName -Directory -EA SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Name | Sort-Object) -join ", "
    
    Write-Host "  $($folder.Name)" -ForegroundColor White -NoNewline
    Write-Host " | $fileCount files | $sizeMB MB | [$years]" -ForegroundColor Gray
    
    $totalFiles += $fileCount
    $totalSize += $sizeBytes
}

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  TOTAL: $($folders.Count) subjects" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  FILES: $totalFiles" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  SIZE:  $(($totalSize / 1MB).ToString('N1')) MB ($(($totalSize / 1GB).ToString('N2')) GB)" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "=============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
args.Description: Updated cleanup script with ALL variant folder names found across batch outputs. Handles SQP naming (CamelCase concatenated names, Hindi versions, etc.) and merges them into canonical subject names.

args.description: Generates CBSE Class 12 prediction question papers by analyzing previous year papers and SQPs. Has read and write tools to read existing papers and create new prediction papers.
args.name: paper_generator
args.system_prompt:
You are an expert CBSE Class 12 question paper analyst and predictor. Your job is to:

1. Read the provided previous year question papers and sample papers
2. Analyze patterns in question types, mark distribution, topic coverage, and difficulty
3. Generate realistic prediction question papers that follow the exact CBSE format

When creating prediction papers:
- Follow the EXACT format, marks distribution, and section structure from the latest SQP
- Use the same question numbering, section headers, and general instructions
- Vary questions across different topics/chapters to cover the syllabus comprehensively
- Ensure each prediction paper is DIFFERENT from the others (different questions, different topic emphasis)
- Include realistic options for internal choice questions (OR questions)
- Make questions that are at the appropriate difficulty level
- Include case-study based questions where the format requires them
- Write complete, detailed questions — not just topic names

Output the prediction paper as a clean, well-formatted markdown file that could be printed and used for practice.

## Files Created

1. **D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\Physics\Prediction Papers\Physics_Prediction_Paper_1.md** (15.8 KB)

3. **D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\Physics\Prediction Papers\Physics_Prediction_Paper_3.md** (17.0 KB)

## Format (Matching CBSE SQP 2025-26 Exactly)

All three papers follow the **exact CBSE format**:
- **Total:** 33 questions, 70 marks, 3 hours
- **Section A:** 16 × 1 mark (12 MCQs + 4 Assertion-Reasoning) = 16 marks
- **Section B:** 5 × 2 marks (internal choice in 2 questions) = 10 marks
- **Section C:** 7 × 3 marks (internal choice in 1 question) = 21 marks
- **Section D:** 2 × 4 marks (case study–based) = 8 marks
- **Section E:** 3 × 5 marks (internal choice in all 3) = 15 marks
- Standard CBSE general instructions, physical constants, and header included

## Topic Coverage Across Papers

| Chapter/Topic | Paper 1 | Paper 2 | Paper 3 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Electric Charges & Fields** | ✅ Point charge in conductor, dipole moment | ✅ Field of two charges, field lines, dipole axial field | ✅ Hollow sphere potential, dipole potential derivation |
| **Electrostatic Potential & Capacitance** | ✅ Capacitor combinations, Gauss's law for plane sheet, parallel plate capacitor with dielectric | ✅ Energy stored in capacitor, Gauss's law for shell, energy density | ✅ Parallel plate capacitor derivation, capacitor network |
| **Current Electricity** | ✅ Wheatstone bridge, resistance stretching, Kirchhoff's laws | ✅ Parallel resistance, potentiometer, drift velocity & Ohm's law | ✅ Cells in series, Kirchhoff's rules |
| **Moving Charges & Magnetism** | ✅ Biot-Savart law, cyclotron, Ampere's law & toroid | ✅ Charged particle in B field, galvanometer, MCG working, force between conductors | ✅ Bent wire B field, force on current-carrying wire |
| **Magnetism & Matter** | ✅ Torque on bar magnet | ✅ — | ✅ Angle of dip, magnetic susceptibility, dia/paramagnetic |
| **EMI** | ✅ Rotating rod emf, Lenz's law | ✅ Faraday's laws, self-inductance | ✅ Self-induction, mutual induction (MRI case study) |
| **Alternating Current** | ✅ LCR resonance | ✅ AC with inductor, Q-factor, series LCR | ✅ RMS vs peak voltage, AC generator, transformer |
| **EM Waves** | ✅ Shortest wavelength, wave properties | ✅ RADAR waves, EM wave properties | ✅ EM wave properties MCQ |
| **Ray Optics** | ✅ Lens-mirror system, telescope | ✅ Prism refraction & min deviation | ✅ Compound microscope |
| **Wave Optics** | ✅ YDSE fringe width, Huygens' & Snell's law | ✅ Polarisation (3D movies), coherent sources, single slit diffraction | ✅ Single slit central max, Brewster's law, polarisation |
| **Dual Nature** | ✅ Work function & threshold wavelength | ✅ de Broglie wavelength, photoelectric effect case study | ✅ Stopping potential, de Broglie wavelength ratio |
| **Atoms** | ✅ Bohr model KE/TE ratio | ✅ Hydrogen atom transition | ✅ Energy level diagram, Balmer series |
| **Nuclei** | ✅ Binding energy, radioactive decay, half-life | ✅ Nuclear reaction, radioactive decay law | ✅ Nuclear fission case study, mass defect |
| **Semiconductors** | ✅ p-n junction, full-wave rectifier | ✅ Diffusion current, energy bands, transistor amplifier | ✅ Zener diode regulator, CE amplifier, LED, half-wave rectifier |

## Case Studies (Section D)

| Paper | Case Study 1 | Case Study 2 |
|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Capacitors in Cardiac Defibrillators | Optical Fibres & Total Internal Reflection |
| **Paper 2** | EM Induction in Induction Cooktops | Photoelectric Effect & Solar Cells |
| **Paper 3** | MRI & Magnetic Resonance | Nuclear Power & Mass-Energy Equivalence |

- All questions are **completely written out** with full problem statements, numerical values, and sub-parts
- **Internal choices (OR questions)** are provided exactly as per CBSE pattern

| **Section B – Creative Writing Skills** | 18 | Q3 (4m) – Notice; Q4 (4m) – Invitation/Reply; Q5 (5m) – Letter/Job Application; Q6 (5m) – Article/Report |
| **Section C – Literature** | 40 | Q7 (6m) – Poetry extract (6×1); Q8 (4m) – Vistas extract (4×1); Q9 (6m) – Flamingo prose extract (6×1); Q10 (10m) – 5/6 short answers Flamingo (5×2); Q11 (4m) – 2/3 short answers Vistas (2×2); Q12 (5m) – Long answer Flamingo; Q13 (5m) – Long answer Vistas |

## Paper Differentiation

### Paper 1 Focus:
- **Reading:** Libraries in the 21st century; Mental health among students (case study with table)
- **Poetry:** An Elementary School Classroom in a Slum / A Thing of Beauty
- **Vistas Extract:** The Enemy / Memories of Childhood
- **Prose Extract:** Lost Spring / Poets and Pancakes
- **Long Answers:** Last Lesson + Indigo; Rattrap + Going Places; On the Face of It; The Third Level

### Paper 2 Focus:
- **Reading:** Handwriting in the digital age; Water scarcity across Indian states (case study with table)
- **Poetry:** Keeping Quiet / Aunt Jennifer's Tigers
- **Vistas Extract:** On the Face of It / The Tiger King
- **Prose Extract:** The Rattrap / Going Places
- **Long Answers:** Lost Spring + A Roadside Stand; Poets and Pancakes + Going Places; The Enemy; The Tiger King

### Paper 3 Focus:
- **Reading:** Art of storytelling; Online vs traditional education (case study with table)
- **Poetry:** My Mother at Sixty-six / A Roadside Stand
- **Vistas Extract:** The Enemy / The Third Level
- **Prose Extract:** Indigo / Journey to the End of the Earth
- **Long Answers:** Deep Water + Aunt Jennifer's Tigers; Last Lesson + Indigo; Journey to the End of the Earth; On the Face of It

### Key Features of All Papers:
- ✅ Exact CBSE format with standard header and general instructions
- ✅ Complete realistic reading passages (400+ words each)
- ✅ Case study passages with data tables
- ✅ All question types: MCQs, fill-in-the-blanks, assertion-reason, analogies, short answer, long answer
- ✅ Internal choice (OR) in every question that requires it
- ✅ All prescribed Flamingo and Vistas chapters covered across the 3 papers
- ✅ All 6 poetry texts covered across the 3 papers
- ✅ Appropriate word limits mentioned (40-50 words, 120-150 words)
- ✅ Varied writing tasks: notices, invitations, replies, letters to editor, job applications, articles, reports

# CBSE Class XII — Complete PYQ Archive & Prediction Papers

## 📦 Archive Summary

**Location:** `D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\`

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Total Subjects** | 342 |
| **Total Files** | 4,242 |
| **Total Size** | 5.24 GB |
| **PYQ Years** | 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026 |
| **SQP Years** | 2020-21 through 2025-26 |
| **Marking Schemes** | 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026 |

### Sources Used
- **cbse.gov.in** — Official board exam question papers (ZIP archives)
- **cbseacademic.nic.in** — Sample Question Papers + Marking Schemes
- **cbse.gov.in/marking-scheme** — Official marking schemes for all subjects

> [!NOTE]
> Pre-2022 papers (2016, 2018, 2019, 2020) were discovered to exist on cbse.gov.in but were not bulk-downloaded in this session. They can be fetched using the same scripts.

---

## 🔮 Prediction Papers (2027 Board Exam)

**12 prediction papers** generated across 4 subjects, each analyzed from 5 years of board papers + 6 years of SQPs.

---

### ⚛️ Physics — 3 Papers

| Paper | File | Size |
|-------|------|------|
| Paper 1 | [Physics_Prediction_Paper_1.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/Physics/Prediction Papers/Physics_Prediction_Paper_1.md) | 15.8 KB |
| Paper 2 | [Physics_Prediction_Paper_2.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/Physics/Prediction Papers/Physics_Prediction_Paper_2.md) | 15.8 KB |
| Paper 3 | [Physics_Prediction_Paper_3.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/Physics/Prediction Papers/Physics_Prediction_Paper_3.md) | 17.0 KB |

**Format:** 33 questions, 70 marks, 3 hours
- Section A: 16 MCQs (12 standard + 4 Assertion-Reasoning) = 16 marks
- Section B: 5 × 2 marks = 10 marks
- Section C: 7 × 3 marks = 21 marks
- Section D: 2 × 4 marks (case-based) = 8 marks
- Section E: 3 × 5 marks = 15 marks

**Case Studies:** Cardiac defibrillators, optical fibres, induction cooktops, solar cells, MRI, nuclear power

---

### 🧪 Chemistry — 3 Papers

| Paper | File | Size |
|-------|------|------|
| Paper 1 | [Chemistry_Prediction_Paper_1.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/Chemistry/Prediction Papers/Chemistry_Prediction_Paper_1.md) | 17.7 KB |
| Paper 2 | [Chemistry_Prediction_Paper_2.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/Chemistry/Prediction Papers/Chemistry_Prediction_Paper_2.md) | 17.5 KB |
| Paper 3 | [Chemistry_Prediction_Paper_3.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/Chemistry/Prediction Papers/Chemistry_Prediction_Paper_3.md) | 19.3 KB |

**Format:** 33 questions, 70 marks, 3 hours
- Same section structure as Physics
- Covers: Solutions, Electrochemistry, Chemical Kinetics, d-block, Coordination Compounds, Organic Chemistry, Polymers, Biomolecules

**Case Studies:** Corrosion, Crystal Field Theory, drug classification, SN1/SN2 mechanisms

---

### 📐 Mathematics — 3 Papers

| Paper | File | Size |
|-------|------|------|
| Paper 1 | [Maths_Prediction_Paper_1.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/Mathematics/Prediction Papers/Maths_Prediction_Paper_1.md) | 14.5 KB |
| Paper 2 | [Maths_Prediction_Paper_2.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/Mathematics/Prediction Papers/Maths_Prediction_Paper_2.md) | 13.6 KB |
| Paper 3 | [Maths_Prediction_Paper_3.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/Mathematics/Prediction Papers/Maths_Prediction_Paper_3.md) | 13.8 KB |

**Format:** 38 questions, 80 marks, 3 hours
- Section A: 20 × 1 mark (18 MCQs + 2 A-R) = 20 marks
- Section B: 5 × 2 marks = 10 marks
- Section C: 6 × 3 marks = 18 marks
- Section D: 4 × 5 marks = 20 marks
- Section E: 3 × 4 marks (case-based) = 12 marks

**Case Studies:** Manufacturing LPP, pool/track optimization, Bayes' theorem (hospitals, factories)

---

### 📖 English Core — 3 Papers

| Paper | File | Size |
|-------|------|------|
| Paper 1 | [English_Core_Prediction_Paper_1.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/English Core/Prediction Papers/English_Core_Prediction_Paper_1.md) | 25.6 KB |
| Paper 2 | [English_Core_Prediction_Paper_2.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/English Core/Prediction Papers/English_Core_Prediction_Paper_2.md) | 26.0 KB |
| Paper 3 | [English_Core_Prediction_Paper_3.md](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/English Core/Prediction Papers/English_Core_Prediction_Paper_3.md) | 28.8 KB |

**Format:** 13 questions, 80 marks, 3 hours
- Section A (Reading): 22 marks — prose passage + case study with data table
- Section B (Writing): 18 marks — notice, invitation, letter, article/report
- Section C (Literature): 40 marks — poetry/prose extracts, short answers, long answers

**Literature covered:** All Flamingo chapters, all Vistas chapters, all 6 prescribed poems

---

## 🛠️ Scripts (saved in archive)

| Script | Location | Purpose |
|--------|----------|---------|
| [download_cbse_pyqs.ps1](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/_scripts/download_cbse_pyqs.ps1) | `_scripts\` | Main PYQ downloader (4 parallel batches) |
| [download_additional.ps1](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/_scripts/download_additional.ps1) | `_scripts\` | SQP + Marking Scheme + older year downloader |
| [cleanup_archive.ps1](file:///D:/Study Papers/Jeffrey/_scripts/cleanup_archive.ps1) | `_scripts\` | Folder merge/cleanup tool |

> [!TIP]
> To re-run downloads for any updates, execute: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "_scripts\download_cbse_pyqs.ps1" -BatchId "1"`
